subject: Issue-ID: VID-835 optimization in git clone using --depth
commit_message:
Issue-ID: VID-835 optimization in git clone using --depth

optimize the git clone using --depth flag in term of size of clone
and also in term's of time taken to fetch the files and commit history
of whole repository.

Respective issue with Issue-ID: VID-835 is created at

https://jira.onap.org/browse/VID-835

More detail can be found at blog
https://www.atlassian.com/git/tutorials/big-repositories

Signed-off-by: Pratik Raj <rajpratik71@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I3e5b4692997c139538103364e336f17fd21be29e

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/vid-master-verify-java/2754

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

This commit is irrelevant, as it changes a documentation. It will be declined later on, if no justification will be provided.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> 
> This commit is irrelevant, as it changes a documentation. It will be declined later on, if no justification will be provided.

it is just a optimization tweak for git cloning , with --depth flag only shallow clone the repo which reduces size and time of clones.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > This commit is irrelevant, as it changes a documentation. It will be declined later on, if no justification will be provided.
> 
> it is just a optimization tweak for git cloning , with --depth flag only shallow clone the repo which reduces size and time of clones.

You modified an doc example not an actual clone.
User should be able to decide his shallow needs by his own.
